title: Ewan McGregor
content:
{{Top|rwp}} {{Person |image=[[File:EwanMcGregor.jpg]] |name=Ewan McGregor |birth=[[March 31]], [[1971]]
[[Wikipedia:Perth and Kinross|Perth and Kinross]], [[Wikipedia:Scotland|Scotland]]''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, New Edition]]'' |death= |listedpronouns= |nationality=[[Wikipedia:Scottish people|Scottish]] |occupation=[[Wikipedia:Actor|Actor]] |companies= |website= }} {{Quote|What Episode I star has been called 'the biggest thing out of Scotland since argyle socks'?|An ''Episode I'' preview question from [[Star Wars Trivial Pursuit]] indicating Ewan McGregor|[[Star Wars Trivial Pursuit]]}} '''Ewan Gordon McGregor''' [[Wikipedia:Order of the British Empire|OBE]] (born [[March 31]], [[1971]]) is a [[Wikipedia:Scottish people|Scottish]] film [[Wikipedia:Actor|actor]] who has had significant success in mainstream, independent, and art-house films. He played the [[Jedi]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]] in the [[prequel trilogy]] and the [[Star Wars: Obi-Wan Kenobi|eponymous]] television series. Outwith ''[[Star Wars]]'', McGregor is famed for starring in films such as [[Wikipedia:Trainspotting (film)|''Trainspotting'']] and [[Wikipedia:Moulin Rouge!|''Moulin Rouge!'']] and as a motorcyclist advocating for [[Wikipedia:UNICEF|UNICEF]] programmes. ==Biography== ===Early life=== Ewan McGregor was born on [[March 31]], [[1971]]. He grew up in [[Wikipedia:Crieff|Crieff]], in [[Wikipedia:Perth and Kinross|Perth and Kinross]], [[Wikipedia:Scotland|Scotland]].{{BookCite|author=James Hatfield|title=Ewan McGregor: Rising to the Stars|publisher=[[Berkley Boulevard]]|date=[[1999]]|isbn=0-425-16900-6|quote="Lawson, who played the only other X-wing pilot besides Luke Skywalker to battle the Empire's dark forces and survive, was Ewan's uncle."}} He wanted to be an actor when he was a child{{SWYouTube|ufI9DDBJVGs|Behind the Scenes of the Obi-Wan Kenobi Series!|sw_url=video/behind-the-scenes-of-the-obi-wan-kenobi-series}} and at five years old, McGregor joined his brother Colin at the local boarding school, [[Wikipedia:Morrison's Academy|Morrison's Academy]]. At school, he developed musical talent, playing drums and winning an award for the [[Wikipedia:French horn|French horn]]. He could also sing quite well. He also had a talent for acting, and his first credit came along at six years old as King David in Crieff South Church's ''David and Goliath''. His biggest inspiration for acting came in the form of his uncle, [[Denis Lawson]]. Lawson portrayed [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|Rebellion]] [[Rebel pilot|pilot]] [[Wedge Antilles]] in all three episodes of the [[original trilogy]]. McGregor was a fan of the original trilogy as a child,{{OfficialYouTube|channel=@VanityFair|name=[[Wikipedia:Vanity Fair (magazine)|''Vanity Fair'']]|video=kWpCRYBq6kM|text=Ewan McGregor, Hayden Christensen & Moses Ingram Answer 7 Star Wars Questions | Vanity Fair|archivedate=20220518174951}} and he and his brother would watch the films on video until it was worn out. McGregor was amazed by [[Alec Guinness]]' portrayal as [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]],''[[Obi-Wan Kenobi: A Jedi's Return]]'' and he wanted to be part of ''[[Star Wars]]''. At the age of nine, McGregor decided he wanted to become an actor. ===The prequel trilogy=== {{Quote|George Lucas wanted to do something very different with the prequels. That's why people felt cheated. It was upsetting when people would laugh and joke about it. Now, many years later, the prequels meant a lot to the generation that were kids then. So from smirking, cynical opinions, now I'm getting feedback from the kids they were made for. I'm really happy about that.|Ewan McGregor on the prequel trilogy|{{WebCite|url=https://screenrant.com/ewan-mcgregor-star-wars-prequels-reactions-response|text=Ewan McGregor Explains How Reaction to Star Wars Prequels Has Changed|author=Ana Dumaraog|date=2019-02-01|work=[[Wikipedia:Screen Rant|''Screen Rant'']]|archivedate=20190201162034}}}} Ewan McGregor portrayed Kenobi in [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']],{{Film|I}} When he began the casting process, he thought that ''Star Wars'' did not feel right to him. He was skeptical about playing Kenobi and thought "Well, I'm this independent, urban-y, grungy actor over here. I'm not this kind of guy."{{WebCite|url=https://ew.com/tv/obi-wan-kenobi-ewan-mcgregor-hayden-christensen-cover-story|text=Inside the 17-year journey to reunite Ewan McGregor and Hayden Christensen for ''Obi-Wan Kenobi''|author=Dalton Ross|date=2022-03-10|work=''[[Wikipedia:Entertainment Weekly|Entertainment Weekly]]''|archivedate=20220310151626}} McGregor did a lot of thinking and research, and he asked his acquaintance if it was the right thing to do. Joining ''Star Wars'' was a difficult decision for McGregor,{{WebCite|url=https://www.vanityfair.com/hollywood/2022/05/star-wars-cover-the-rebellion-will-be-televised|text=''Star Wars:'' The Rebellion Will Be Televised|author=Anthony Breznican|date=2022-05-17|work=[[Wikipedia:Vanity Fair (magazine)|''Vanity Fair'']]|archivedate=20220517110251}} but his anxiety eventually turned into excitement as he got closer to being cast as Kenobi. In [[December]] [[1996]], the actor did an audition interview in [[Wikipedia:London|London]]. Shortly after that, McGregor was told that the role was offered to him. McGregor was excited to play a younger Alec Guinness,{{SWYouTube|ZHEDtlCcnrs|A Series of Firsts | Obi-Wan Kenobi | Disney+|sw_url=video/a-series-of-firsts-obi-wan-kenobi}} and he watched several films with Guinness as a young man to imagine him as a younger person. He also studied Guinness and watched the original trilogy as preparation, but he did not really think about the genre.{{WebCite|url=https://ew.com/tv/star-wars-obi-wan-kenobi-darth-vader-hayden-christensen-clone-wars-rebels|text=Hayden Christensen binged ''Star Wars'' animated shows to prepare for ''Obi-Wan Kenobi''|author=Dalton Ross|date=2022-04-19|work=''[[Wikipedia:Entertainment Weekly|Entertainment Weekly]]''|archivedate=20220419153635}} Director [[George Lucas]] showed McGregor around the studio and showed him the [[Tribubble bongo|submarine]] that he would ride in. After that, they went to the props-making department, and the prop master showed him a chest of [[lightsaber]] props. McGregor then got to choose which [[Obi-Wan Kenobi's first lightsaber|lightsaber]] he would use in the film. The first lightsaber work he did was when he trained for the [[Lightsaber duel|duel]] against [[Darth]] [[Maul]]. He thought it was an amazing thing to be part of, and he found the duels to be very intricate and fast. To show a [[padawan braid]], McGregor had a braid that was stuck in his [[hair]] all day, but he liked the look of it.{{OfficialYouTube|channel=@entertainmentweekly|name=Entertainment Weekly|link=Wikipedia:Entertainment Weekly|video=nYRcBE17jks|text=Ewan McGregor on Obi-Wan Kenobi's Ever Changing Hair Styles | Entertainment Weekly|archivedate=20220622010438}} McGregor said he enjoyed working with Lucas and fellow actors [[Natalie Portman]], [[Liam Neeson]], and [[Ahmed Best]], who played [[Padmé Amidala]], [[Qui-Gon Jinn]], and [[Jar Jar Binks]], respectively. Six to eight [[Standard month|months]] after principal photography of ''The Phantom Menace'', McGregor and other actors returned for re-shoots. He was working on something else, so he could not cut his hair or grow a beard. The crew made a short hair wig for McGregor, but he found it difficult to hide longer hair under a wig. After ''The Phantom Menace'' received negative reviews, McGregor looked down on the franchise and had a hard time dealing with the reception knowing he had to be in more prequel films. [[File:AOTC-McGregortraining.png|thumb|left|250px|Ewan McGregor practices lightsaber combat with Stunt Coordinator Nick Gillard during production on ''Attack of the Clones'']] McGregor portrayed an older Kenobi in [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']]{{Film|II}} Shortly after [[Hayden Christensen]], the actor who played [[Anakin Skywalker]], was picked by George Lucas, he felt nervous as he arrived at a soundstage in [[Wikipedia:Sydney|Sydney]], [[Wikipedia:Australia|Australia]]. He walked into the [[hair]]-and-makeup room to see his fellow actor for the first time. McGregor greeted Christensen as if they had been lifelong friends. He then welcomed Christensen into the family and helped ease Christensen's transition into the huge [[Geonosis droid factories|droid-factory]]-like machinery of the ''Star Wars'' [[universe]]. McGregor and Christensen practiced lightsaber rehearsals, which Christensen said was the most fun he had, and he described it as "playing with your friend every day." He also had lightsaber training with stunt coordinator [[Nick Gillard]]. McGregor did not like Kenobi's hairstyle in the film, but he figured that it was supposed to show progression. McGregor found it challenging to realistically speak to the [[Kaminoan]]s because they were not really there. McGregor also portrayed Kenobi in [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']].{{Film|III}} It was McGregor's idea for he and the crew to look at pictures of Alec Guinness in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] to bridge the gap between the two actors. Also, his hair and beard were grayed a little bit to get closer to Guinness's portrayal. During filming, McGregor met by chance [[Ross Beadman]], the child actor who played an ill-fated [[Unidentified Jedi youngling|Jedi youngling]], taking the chance to speak with him for ten minutes and invite him and his mother for [[Wikipedia:Champagne|champagne]].{{Reddit|url=r/IAmA/comments/jwmi7f/im_ross_beadman_the_actor_who_played_the_jedi|thread=I'm Ross Beadman. The actor who played the Jedi youngling in Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith who spoke to and was killed by Anakin Skywalker! Ask me anything…|user=[[Ross Beadman|RossBandeam]]|date=2020-11-18|quote=I was closest with Ewan McGregor as me and my mum spoke to him the most even if it was for 10 minutes. My mum pointed him out to me and I didn't recognise him at first until she said: Ross..it's Obi-Wan!". Then he said hello to us and we shared champagne with him. Definitely my biggest highlight!|archivedate=20220701215638}} He said that the [[prequel trilogy]] presented difficulty due to "walking around with the big alien people that aren't really there and spending a lot of time on a blue set just speaking into the air," but he also said that there were aspects that he viewed as "mind-blowing." The prequel trilogy was subjected to much criticism from audiences, which McGregor said was difficult to deal with. He also did not know how to deal with the criticism because he was early in his career. McGregor was relieved to put the franchise behind him. ===''Star Wars'' semi-hiatus=== Since the news about the ''Star Wars'' [[sequel trilogy]] were announced in [[2012]], McGregor expressed interest in coming back to the series if asked to. He made a brief audio appearance in the [[2015]] film [[Star Wars: Episode VII The Force Awakens|''Star Wars'': Episode VII ''The Force Awakens'']] as Kenobi's voice calling out to [[Rey Skywalker|Rey]] in her [[Force vision|Force-induced visions]]. Voice actor [[James Arnold Taylor]], who voiced McGregor's character in different ''Star Wars'' media ranging from animation to [[Star Wars video games|video game]]s, originally recorded the dialogue, but his recordings were replaced with McGregor's, a decision Taylor found logical and was fine with.{{Twitter|jatactor|status/678299784621350912|James Arnold Taylor|quote=@t_betts94 yes, it was me originally but they replaced my version with Ewan. I guess if I'm gonna be replaced it should be him! 😀|archivedate=20190828225746}} Following the ''[[Star Wars Rebels]]'' [[Star Wars Rebels Season Two|second season's]] [[Twilight of the Apprentice|finale]], ''Rebels'' showrunner [[Dave Filoni]] expressed his interest in having McGregor voice Obi-Wan Kenobi in the show despite Arnold Taylor's availability, being willing to bring back any ''Star Wars'' actors to reprise their movie roles in the show.{{WebCite|url=https://dorksideoftheforce.com/2016/05/21/dave-filoni-wants-ewan-mcgregor-to-voice-obi-wan-on-star-wars-rebels|text=Dave Filoni Wants Ewan McGregor To Voice Obi Wan On Star Wars Rebels|author=Elaine Tveit|date=2022-05-21|work=[https://dorksideoftheforce.com Dork Side of the Force]|archivedate=20160522121507}} [[Stephen Stanton]] would ultimately voice Kenobi when he appeared in the [[Star Wars Rebels Season Three|third season]] episode "[[Twin Suns (episode)|Twin Suns]]."{{Rebels|Twin Suns (episode)}} ===''Obi-Wan Kenobi'' television series=== {{Quote|It was nice to come back and to bridge that gap between me and Alec Guinness. I got as much, or more, out of playing him this time than I did in the first three movies put together. That's to do with the writing, and the people we were making the series with, and the technology, and how different everything is. If we were to get an opportunity to do it again, I'd be totally up for that.|Ewan McGregor|{{WebCite|url=https://www.ign.com/articles/obi-wan-ewan-mcgregor-got-more-out-of-disney-plus-return-than-first-three-movies-put-together|text=Obi-Wan: Ewan McGregor Got More Out Of Disney Plus Return Than 'First Three Movies Put Together'|author=Mike Williams|date=2022-04-27|work=[[Wikipedia:IGN|''IGN'']]|archivedate=20220427233410}}}} McGregor knew about an Obi-Wan Kenobi spin-off project as far back as 2015,{{WebCite|url=https://www.cbr.com/obi-wan-kenobi-mcgregor-kept-series-secret-longer|text=Obi-Wan Kenobi: McGregor Kept the Series a Secret Longer Than You Think|author=Samuel Gelman|date=2012-10-24|work=[[Wikipedia:Comic Book Resources|''Comic Book Resources'']]|archivedate=20200107023840}} and until [[2019]], he was forced to withhold information in interviews.{{WebCite|url=https://www.mensjournal.com/features/ewan-mcgregor-cover-story|text=Ewan McGregor Is Back on Top—Again|author=Mickey Rapkin|date=2019-10-24|work=[[Wikipedia:Men's Journal|''Men's Journal'']]|archivedate=20200727012231}} In [[2016]], McGregor said that he did not have a burning need to play Kenobi again and he said he did not have any fascination for the ''Star Wars'' movies. He said it was possibly because he had already been in the movies and had "seen behind the curtain." He added that the movies did not "have the same type of wonderment" to him. McGregor met many people who were kids when the prequels were released who expressed how important the films were to them. He enjoyed the "wave of positivity" and praise for his role as Kenobi, which sparked something in him and he realised he also loved his role. In interviews, McGregor stated that he was open to returning as Kenobi in ''Star Wars'', but he worried he was too open. He told people he'd be happy to play Kenobi if Lucasfilm wanted, and people thought that he was "touting for work at [[The Walt Disney Company|Disney's]] door." It was publicly believed that McGregor was happy to play Kenobi again. In [[2018]], a meeting was held to see how serious the actor was to play Kenobi in an Obi-Wan Kenobi film as part of a [[Star Wars Anthology Series|series]] of stand-alone movies outside of the [[Star Wars saga|Skywalker saga]]. [[Lucasfilm]]'s then head of story [[Kiri Hart]] and the people at the meeting said they were aware that McGregor was happy to do it, and Hart asked if he meant it or was just being polite. They asked if McGregor was in or not and he said he was. Lucasfilm then actively began development on an Obi-Wan Kenobi film that would be directed by [[Stephen Daldry]]. McGregor would be the producer, giving him more say over the story. He wanted Kenobi to start as a broken, faithless man and the film would show what gets him back together again. At some point, McGregor did some testing for the film.{{WebCite|url=https://www.gamesradar.com/ewan-mcgregor-donned-obi-wan-kenobis-costume-on-the-set-of-the-mandalorian|text=Ewan McGregor donned Obi-Wan Kenobi's costume on the set of The Mandalorian|author=Alyssa Mercante|date=2020-10-27|work=[[Wikipedia:GamesRadar+|''GamesRadar+'']]|archivedate=20201027114511}} [[File:ObiWanKenobi-OWKPoster.png|thumb|right|190px|Obi-Wan Kenobi character poster for ''Obi-Wan Kenobi'']] The film eventually became a television series directed by [[Deborah Chow]] who McGregor was excited to work with. McGregor was very involved throughout the whole development. At some point, he did camera tests wearing Kenobi's costume on the set of ''[[Star Wars: The Mandalorian]]''. To prepare for portraying Kenobi again, McGregor watched all nine movies in the Skywalker saga, which was the first time he watched the prequels since they were originally released. He also started reading [[Wikipedia:Science fiction|science fiction]], including books by [[Wikipedia:Iain Banks|Iain M. Banks]]. McGregor felt like getting dressed as Kenobi was a very special moment. He had a very fun time playing Kenobi again, and he enjoyed bridging the gap between his portrayal and that of Sir Alec Guinness. To relearn his Kenobi voice, McGregor listened to a sound file that has all of Guinness' lines from ''A New Hope''.{{OfficialYouTube|channel=@RottenTomatoesTV|name=Rotten Tomatoes|link=Wikipedia:Rotten Tomatoes|video=K-BiOMIR6NM|text='Obi-Wan Kenobi' Star Ewan McGregor Explains Why He Sounds So 'Alec Guinness-y' | Rotten Tomatoes TV|Rotten Tomatoes TV]]|archivedate=20220527155120}} McGregor also imagined how Guinness would say the lines before filming a scene. When the crew was happy with a scene, McGregor would do another take that was an over-the-top Guinness version.{{YouTube|video=xQ7OZxXNgKA|text=Star Wars Spin-Off OBI-WAN KENOBI: Ewan McGregor, Hayden Christensen, Moses Ingram & Deborah Chow|channel=@KinoPlus|name=Kino Plus|archivedate=20220522190720}} McGregor wanted Kenobi to have an unkempt hairstyle and appearance to represent how Kenobi is faithless and somewhat given up. When McGregor was playing Kenobi, he made it seem like the character would grow up to be Guinness by trying to sound and feel like Guinness, but he did not want to do an impersonation. He said he got more out of playing Kenobi in the show than he did in the prequel trilogy altogether because of the writing, people he worked with, the technology, and how different everything was. Christensen said that the first time he saw McGregor as Kenobi was a very special moment that he'd remember for a very long time. In a chemistry test between McGregor and [[Vivien Lyra Blair]], the actress who played [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|Leia Organa]] in the series, they recited the lines for one of their conversations on [[Daiyu]]. [[File:TethaGrigBehindTheScenes.png|thumb|left|240px|Ewan McGregor and his daughter [[Esther-Rose McGregor|Esther-Rose]] on the Daiyu set]] McGregor enjoyed working with Deborah Chow and said that she had such quiet authority and fully understood the ''Star Wars'' world. McGregor also liked how Chow was able to merge the series with the things that were made on set in [[1975]] and what actors said in [[2003]].{{WebCite|url=https://ew.com/tv/obi-wan-kenobi-darth-vader-star-wars-line|text=How ''Obi-Wan Kenobi'' changes the meaning behind a classic ''Star Wars'' line|author=Dalton Ross|date=2022-03-26|work=''[[Wikipedia:Entertainment Weekly|Entertainment Weekly]]''|archivedate=20220426153238}} On the first day of production, McGregor starting filming his scenes in the [[Tibidon Station|Sandwhale Farm]] set. [[Moses Ingram]], the actress who played the [[Reva Sevander|Third Sister]], received fight training from McGregor whom she described as "a graceful little butterfly." He showed her encouragement as she fumbled through rookie mistakes. One day, McGregor was shooting a scene and he dropped [[Obi-Wan Kenobi's third lightsaber|his lightsaber]] in the crevice between the virtual LED set known as the Volume and where the stage ends. The first scene shot with Christensen was when he played Anakin Skywalker in a vision seen by Kenobi. The scene was shot on location, and McGregor insisted on staying on the set to be part of Christensen's first shot back.{{WebCite|url=https://www.cinemablend.com/interviews/obi-wan-kenobis-hayden-christensen-talks-wearing-the-anakin-costume-again-and-ewan-mcgregors-sweet-gesture|text=Obi-Wan Kenobi's Hayden Christensen Talks Wearing The Anakin Costume Again And Ewan McGregor's 'Sweet' Gesture|author=Laura Hurley|date=2022-06-15|work=[https://www.cinemablend.com Cinemablend]|archivedate=20220615135029}} [[File:HaydenChristensenandEwanMcGregorTraining.png|thumb|right|220px|Ewan McGregor and Hayden Christensen training with lightsabers for their training scene in "Part V"]] McGregor said that Blair had all of the wit and cheek of [[Carrie Fisher]]'s portrayal of Organa in the original trilogy. McGregor and Organa got to know each other better during shooting, which went hand in hand with Kenobi and Organa's relationship in the series. When McGregor rehearsed the [[Battle on Mapuzo|duel on Mapuzo]], Vader was not wearing the [[helmet]]. When the scene was filmed, Vader came from behind McGregor, giving him a jolt of fear. McGregor also said that seeing [[stormtrooper]]s made him feel like he was seven years old.{{WebCite|url=https://www.gamesradar.com/ewan-mcgregor-obi-wan-kenobi-darth-vader-scared|text=Ewan McGregor: "I turned around, and f**king Darth Vader was coming at me. It scared the s**t out of me"|author=Jack Shepherd|date=2022-04-25|work=[[Wikipedia:GamesRadar+|''GamesRadar+'']]|archivedate=20220425140226}} During long days of filming, McGregor asked movement coordinator [[Olga Sokolova]] for a good warm-up, so she had him do a technical contemporary warm-up. Sokolova was amazed by McGregor's physical abilities after seeing him smoothly go through advanced moves.{{Instagram|username=olgamovement|url=p/CemUDJFO33a|name=Olga Sokolova|date=2022-06-09|quote="Jedi Tombs" scene from @obiwankenobi episode 4 was one of my favorite to work on, thanks to director Deborah Chow for trusting me this special piece✨ During long days of filming Ewan asked me to give him a good warmup and I thought technical contemporary warmup would be the best ✨ I was so amazed by Ewan's physical abilities after seeing him smoothly go through advanced technical moves, what a master|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/w9pkN}} McGregor and Christensen trained with lightsabers for the flashback of Kenobi and Skywalker training in the [[Part V|fifth episode]].{{Kenobi|Part V}} They both had dots on their face for the de-aging technology. McGregor thought it was incredible to see Liam Neeson reprise his role as Qui-Gon Jinn in the [[Part VI|sixth episode]].{{Kenobi|Part VI}} McGregor said he'd be play Kenobi again if he got the opportunity. On [[August 23]], 2019,{{SW|url=news/the-galaxy-far-far-away-just-got-a-little-bigger|text=The Galaxy Far, Far Away Just Got A Little Bigger…}} McGregor appeared on stage at the [[Wikipedia:D23_(Disney)#2019|D23 convention]] and announced that he would be reprising his role as Kenobi in the ''[[Star Wars: Obi-Wan Kenobi]]'' television series on the [[Disney+]] streaming service.{{SW|url=news/d23-expo-2019-8-things-we-learned-about-star-wars-at-the-disney-showcase|text=D23 Expo 2019: 8 Things We Learned About ''Star Wars'' at the Disney+ Showcase}} The series is set [[19 BBY|ten years]] after the events of ''Revenge of the Sith''.{{ILM|url=vfx/obi-wan-kenobi|text=Obi-Wan Kenobi}} The series was originally going to start streaming on [[May 25]], [[2022]].{{SWYouTube|TWTfhyvzTx0|Obi-Wan Kenobi | Teaser Trailer | Disney+}} On [[March 21]], 2022, McGregor announced that the premiere would be pushed back to [[May 27]], 2022, and the first two episodes would premiere together.{{SWYouTube|gEuirQWecEw|Obi-Wan Kenobi | Announcement | Disney+|sw_url=video/obi-wan-kenobi-announcement}} He was interviewed for ''[[Obi-Wan Kenobi: A Jedi's Return]]'', a documentary about the making of the series. ==Works== ===Filmography=== {|{{Prettytable}} ! Year !! Title !! Series !! Contribution(s) |- |[[1999]]||[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']]|| ||[[Obi-Wan Kenobi]] |- |[[2002]]||[[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']]|| ||Obi-Wan Kenobi |- |[[2005]]||[[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']]|| ||Obi-Wan Kenobi |- |[[2015]]||[[Star Wars: Episode VII The Force Awakens|''Star Wars'': Episode VII ''The Force Awakens'']]|| ||Voiced Obi-Wan Kenobi{{Twitter|jatactor|status/678299784621350912|James Arnold Taylor|quote=@t_betts94 yes, it was me originally but they replaced my version with Ewan. I guess if I'm gonna be replaced it should be him! 😀|archivedate=20190828225746}} |- |[[2019]]||"[[Yoda vs Count Dooku – Size Matters Not]]"||''[[Star Wars Galaxy of Adventures]]''||Voiced Obi-Wan Kenobi |- |2019||"[[Jedi vs. Sith - The Skywalker Saga]]"||''Star Wars Galaxy of Adventures''||Voiced Obi-Wan Kenobi |- |2019||[[Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker|''Star Wars'': Episode IX ''The Rise of Skywalker'']]|| ||Voiced Obi-Wan Kenobi |- |[[2022]]||"[[Part I]]"||''[[Star Wars: Obi-Wan Kenobi]]''||Obi-Wan Kenobi, Executive Producer |- |2022||"[[Part II]]"||''Obi-Wan Kenobi''||Obi-Wan Kenobi, Executive Producer{{Kenobi|Part II}} |- |2022||"[[Part III]]"||''Obi-Wan Kenobi''||Obi-Wan Kenobi, Executive Producer |- |2022||"[[Part IV]]"||''Obi-Wan Kenobi''||Obi-Wan Kenobi, Executive Producer |- |2022||"[[Part V]]"||''Obi-Wan Kenobi''||Obi-Wan Kenobi, Executive Producer |- |2022||"[[Part VI]]"||''Obi-Wan Kenobi''||Obi-Wan Kenobi, Executive Producer |- |2022||''[[Obi-Wan Kenobi: A Jedi's Return]]''||||Special Thanks to |} ===Bibliography=== {|{{Prettytable}} ! Year !! Title !! Contribution(s) |- |[[2017]]||''[[The Phantom Menace Read-Along Storybook and CD]]''||Voiced [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]] |- |2017||''[[Attack of the Clones Read-Along Storybook and CD]]''||Voiced Obi-Wan Kenobi |- |2017||''[[Revenge of the Sith Read-Along Storybook and CD]]''||Voiced Obi-Wan Kenobi |} ==Sources== {{ScrollBox| {{IncompleteList|oou=1}} *{{SWArchive|url=episode-i/bts/production/f19981216/index.html|text=Trading Lightsaber Blows with Obi-Wan Kenobi}} *[[Star Wars Insider 41|''Star Wars Insider'' 41]] *''[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace Scrapbook]]'' *{{SWArchive|url=episode-i/bts/profile/f19990512/index.html|text=In the Footsteps of Obi-Wan Kenobi}} *{{Film|I}}
